title: Pazhamudircholai Murugan Temple
tagline: Pazhamudircholai Murugan Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Solaimalai Murugan Temple is a Hindu temple, located atop a hill, in the outskirts of Madurai in Tamil Nadu.
overview.description: Solaimalai Murugan Temple is a Hindu temple, located atop a hill, in the outskirts of Madurai in Tamil Nadu. It is dedicated to Murugan, and is one of the six abodes of Murugan (Arupadaiveedu). The temple is maintained and administered by the Hindu Religious and Charitable Endowments Department of the Government of Tamil Nadu.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Alagar Kovil
topPlaces.description: Alagar Kovil is a village in Madurai district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The village is centered around Kallalagar Temple.

topPlaces.name: Kallalagar Temple
topPlaces.description: Kallalagar Temple or Kallazhagar Temple, also known by its own toponym Thirumaliruncholai, is a Hindu temple dedicated to the God Vishnu in Alagar Koyil, a village in Madurai district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed in the Tamil Style of Architecture, the temple is glorified in the Naalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Azhvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshiped as Kallalagar, and his consort Lakshmi as Thirumagal.

topPlaces.name: Badri Narayanan temple
topPlaces.description: Badri Narayanan temple (Tamil: பத்ரி நாராயணன் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple in the Madurai District in the entrance of Alagar Koil in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is built in the style of the Badrinath temple, Uttarakhand.

topPlaces.name: Mangulam
topPlaces.description: Mangulam or Mankulam is a village in Madurai district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located 25 kilometres (16 mi) from Madurai.

topPlaces.name: Vellalapatti
topPlaces.description: A.Vallalapatti is a panchayat town in Melur taluk of Madurai district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is one of the nine panchayat towns in the district.

topPlaces.name: Koviloor, Karaikudi
topPlaces.description: Koviloor is a suburban village to the Port of Karaikudi of Sivaganga District of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It contains Arulmigu Kotravaaleeswara Temple, which is more than 1500 years old.

topPlaces.name: Arumbanur
topPlaces.description: Arumbanur is a census town in Madurai-North, Madurai district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. It is 4.5 by road northeast of the village of Kadachanendal.

Constructed in the Tamil Style of Architecture, the temple is glorified in the Naalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Azhvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshiped as Kallalagar, and his consort Lakshmi as Thirumagal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pazhamudircholai Murugan Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pazhamudircholai Murugan Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pazhamudircholai Murugan Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (32 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (23 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pazhamudircholai Murugan Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pazhamudircholai Murugan Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pazhamudircholai Murugan Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Pazhamudircholai Murugan Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pazhamudircholai Murugan Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Alagar Kovil, Kallalagar Temple, Badri Narayanan temple, Mangulam, Vellalapatti.
